key_insight: The paper turns a hard 3D fourth-order singularly perturbed PDE into simpler coupled second-order problems plus a robust Stokes-type system using a generalized Helmholtz decomposition.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a stable low-order finite element option for thin-layer or boundary-layer dominated problems where standard discretizations can become parameter-sensitive.
method: Decompose the fourth-order problem into two elliptic subproblems and one curl-constrained generalized Stokes-type system.
Use standard linear finite elements for the elliptic subproblems.
Use the MINI element with an added L2 Lagrange-multiplier term to make the Stokes-type discretization robust to the perturbation parameter.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a uniform error estimate of order h^{1/2} with respect to the perturbation parameter, and numerical experiments are used to support the theory.
watch_item: The method is low-order and only proven with a relatively modest uniform convergence rate, so implementation details and mesh resolution may matter for sharp layer capture.
paper_title: A Decoupled Low-Order Conforming Mixed Finite Element Method for a Three-Dimensional Fourth-Order Singularly Perturbed Problem
